name: DGTSTestPackageModality
title: DGTS Test Package Modality
description: CodeSystem used to identify the clinical modality or testing domain associated with a genomic test package.
purpose: Defines the categories of genomic testing services available through the Digital Genomic Test Service (DGTS).

concept.definition: Genomic testing of cancers that form solid tumours in various organs or tissues to identify genetic mutations, guide targeted treatments, predict prognosis or assess risk for inherited cancer syndromes.
Genomic testing of blood cancers to detect specific genetic abnormalities, helping to refine diagnosis, prognosis, treatment strategies and monitoring.
Genetic testing to identify pathogenic variants causing rare genetic disorders, often used in diagnosing conditions inherited from parents or assessing genetic risks in families.
Genetic testing to detect mutations that predispose individuals to cancers passed through families, used to assess cancer risk and inform preventive measures.
Genetic testing to assess how an individual's genetic makeup affects their response to medications, guiding drug selection and dosage to improve efficacy and reduce adverse reactions.

Genetic testing to aid calculation based on multiple genetic variants to estimate an individual's risk of developing complex diseases, often used alongside lifestyle and environmental factors for risk prediction.
Repeat analysis of previously generated genomic data.
Reassessment of previously identified genomic variants
Sample to be stored by the Genomic Laboratory Hub (GLH) for future genetic testing.
